name: Separation Anxiety
description: Separation anxiety is a well-recognised behavioural condition characterised by excessive distress when a dog is left alone or separated from its owner. It has a significant impact on welfare and can lead to self-injury and destructive behaviour.
symptoms: Panting and pacing before owner leaves
treatment_overview: Behavioural modification (systematic desensitisation and counterconditioning) by a qualified behaviourist is the cornerstone. Pharmacological support with fluoxetine or clomipramine, alongside pheromone diffusers, assists behaviour modification.
prevention_tips: Gradual training of independence from puppyhood
Avoid long periods of isolation in young dogs
